title: Monsoon Alert – Heavy Rain and Thunderstorm Warning in 13 States, Know Forecast - Times Bull
article_text:
New Delhi: A spell of storms and rainfall continues to be observed across various parts of the country, leaving the situation quite precarious. The unseasonal rains have caused significant damage to farmers’ Rabi crops. In many regions, the likelihood of any immediate relief from adverse weather conditions appears slim.
The outlook for inclement weather also persists in parts of Eastern India. Here, the rainfall could lead to a drop in temperatures ranging from 2 to 3 degrees Celsius. In certain areas of Southern India, temperatures have already dipped due to the rains. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued rain alerts for several states across the country. You can find state-wise updates regarding the rainfall situation below.
Read More: Monsoon Update – Heavy Rain and Thunderstorm Warning in These States, Power Cut Likely
Read More: Business Idea – Say Goodbye to Financial Struggles, Earn Lakhs from Home, Here’s How
According to the IMD, an alert for heavy rainfall accompanied by thunder and lightning has been issued for Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Jammu & Kashmir, West Bengal, and Sikkim for April 12. Additionally, moderate to heavy rainfall is likely in Odisha, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Kerala, and Arunachal Pradesh.
A warning for moderate to intense rainfall has been issued for Nagaland, Manipur, and Mizoram. The IMD has also issued an alert regarding gusty winds blowing at speeds of 50 to 60 kilometres per hour. People have been advised to refrain from venturing outdoors.
Read More: UIDAI Update – How to Update Your Date of Birth on Your Aadhaar Card, Learn the Process
Read More: AI+ New Gadgets 2026: PulseTab Tablet, NovaPods Beats & Air – Price, Specs & Review
According to the IMD, light clouds are expected to hover over Delhi during the morning hours on April 12. The city is likely to witness a gradual rise in temperatures. The maximum temperature in Delhi tomorrow is projected to reach 33 degrees Celsius, while the minimum temperature is expected to settle at 21 degrees Celsius.
According to the Meteorological Department, strong winds may blow across the state during the day on April 12. On a reassuring note, there is absolutely no likelihood of rainfall. The districts in Uttar Pradesh where severe heat conditions are anticipated tomorrow include Meerut, Aligarh, Mathura, Agra, Bareilly, Firozabad, Mainpuri, Kanpur, Lucknow, Ayodhya, Prayagraj, Varanasi, Gorakhpur, and Deoria.
